megaton-entrance-lucas-actor-front-walk.label: Megaton Lucas deterministic front walk

anchor.source: Fallout3.esm MegatonEntrance LucasSimmsRef FormId:0x1003b46; scripted AiTravel on the exact placed reference

notes: No desktop or input automation: a delayed OpenMW startup script applies real AiTravel locomotion to the exact placed Fallout 3 actor after the exterior is ready.

The dense capture covers the verified unobstructed first leg of the route, keeping Lucas's face, beard, hat, hands, Chinese assault rifle, torso, and gait visible before the uneven Megaton terrain ends the travel segment.

goodsprings-easy-pete-face-close-burst.label: Goodsprings Easy Pete retail FaceGen close burst

anchor.source: FalloutNV.esm EasyPeteRef FormId:0x104c80 at its authored Goodsprings exterior placement

notes: Uses Easy Pete's authored exterior reference and native FaceGen/bodymod assets; no staged replacement actor.
Flat close-up colour and head-part gate; package relocation is disabled so the authored exterior ref stays in frame.

goodsprings-easy-pete-actor-tracked-port.label: Goodsprings Easy Pete actor-tracked retail portrait

anchor.source: FalloutNV.esm EasyPeteRef FormId:0x104c80; live rendered face bounds drive the proof camera

notes: Uses the authored Easy Pete reference and the live Bip01 Head world transform; it does not stage or rotate the actor.

Pins the load-order-adjusted NVDefaultExterior IMGS used by the generated Goodsprings bridge cell; patch 0017 then resolves the current weather's authored IMAD records and executes the retail cinematic stage.

The maintained world-viewer camera owns the native screenshot path. A requested frame is held until the head remains in the portrait safe area and both live hand bones remain below the face for eight consecutive frames; rejected poses do not advance the screenshot schedule. This face/material slice hides the equipped weapon and must not enable legacy bind-pose or hand-bind-frame proof overrides; weapon attachment is a separate gate.

goodsprings-easy-pete-dialogue.label: Goodsprings Easy Pete retail dialogue GUI proof

anchor.source: FalloutNV.esm EasyPeteRef FormId:0x104c80 plus retail GREETING DIAL FormId:0xc8 and INFO FormId:0x104c60

notes: Opens the real GM_Dialogue window through DialogueWindow::setPtr and DialogueManager::startDialogue; it does not inject fake UI text.
The OpenMW log must identify the selected retail DIAL/INFO FormIDs and the native screenshots must contain Easy Pete's retail greeting and topic list.

goodsprings-settler-actor-close-burst.label: Goodsprings settler actor close burst

anchor.source: FalloutNV.esm Goodsprings exterior FormId:0x846ea, focused actor proof around mined GSSettlerAM placement

notes: Focused exterior actor proof slice using engine-authored Goodsprings settler placement; do not stage actors from mined coordinates.
One executable launch should produce six native full-body, face-close, side, top, and street-context screenshots around GSSettlerAM for visual pose/material review.

goodsprings-settler-actor-walkaround.label: Goodsprings settler actor player-camera walkaround

goodsprings-settler-actor-front-walk.label: Goodsprings settler deterministic front walk

anchor.source: FalloutNV.esm Goodsprings GSSettlerAM placement FormId:0x1104f0a; scripted AiTravel on base FormId:0x1104f07

notes: No desktop or input automation: a delayed OpenMW startup script applies the real AiTravel package to the exact FNV actor base after the exterior is ready.
Static camera faces north from the open yard while AiTravel turns the actor south, keeping the authored face, hands, weapon, and torso visible throughout the route.

goodsprings-bighorner-creature-front-wal.label: Goodsprings young bighorner deterministic three-quarter walk

anchor.source: FalloutNV.esm Goodsprings GSBighorner02Ref FormId:0x110706e; scripted AiTravel on the authored placed creature

notes: No desktop or input automation: delayed AiTravel moves the exact authored young bighorner while the engine streams the Goodsprings exterior.
Three-quarter follow framing keeps the torso and legs visible; the rejected direct-front diagnostic hid most of the body behind the animal's head and horns.
